text.title: GitOps — Repository Structure Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Repo layout MUST follow `base/` + `overlays/<env>/` per workload. Flat env-specific copies of manifests are rejected.
rule.rationale: Flat copies drift; base-plus-overlays keeps the differences explicit and small.

rule.statement: Multi-team repos MUST allocate `tenants/<team>/` with its own RBAC + Kustomization. Shared global namespaces across teams are rejected.
rule.rationale: Shared namespaces create cross-team blast radius; tenant folders enforce ownership boundaries.

rule.statement: Promotion from env N to env N+1 MUST be a PR that moves the image tag / chart version. Direct edits to the prod overlay outside a PR are rejected.
rule.rationale: PRs are the only auditable promotion artefact; direct edits bypass review.

rule.statement: Per workload choose Kustomize OR Helm; do not mix Helm-template-then-Kustomize-patch inline. (Cross-tool wrapping via Flux HelmRelease + post-render is acceptable.)
rule.rationale: Inline mixing produces unreadable diffs; pick a primary tool per workload.

rule.statement: Every `overlays/<env>/` MUST contain a README.md describing the env's purpose, approvers, and divergence rationale from base.
rule.rationale: Without a README, future operators reverse-engineer the overlays; README codifies the why.

rule.statement: Skip when single-env single-tenant OR Helm-only with per-env values is sufficient.
rule.rationale: Overlays cost cognitive overhead; below thresholds they are pure overhead.
